content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be rearchitected to treat unlabeled, high-value memory traces as analogous to a curious agent’s unexplored environment, using curiosity-driven exploration intrinsic rewards to prioritize which memory traces the agent actively retrains on, instead of only using static uncertainty sampling. This creates a self-reinforcing memory curation loop where the agent actively seeks out under-explored, high-information memories to improve its generalization over time.
